title: File I/O for CLI Tools
content:
CLI tools live and breathe file I/O. Rust's std::fs module provides the basics, and the standard Read/Write traits give you flexible streaming I/O.

Key patterns: read_to_string for small files, BufReader for line-by-line processing of large files, BufWriter for efficient output. Always use the ? operator with context (via anyhow) for clear error messages.

A common CLI idiom: accept '-' as a filename to mean stdin/stdout, allowing your tool to work in Unix pipelines.

codeExamples.description: Reading files with good error messages

keyPoints: Use fs::read_to_string for small files, BufReader for large ones
BufWriter batches writes for much better performance
Always add .with_context() for clear error messages with file paths
Support stdin ('-') for Unix pipeline compatibility
Use Path and PathBuf instead of raw strings for file paths
BufRead::lines() returns Result for each line, handling encoding errors
